content:
If you have changed password for BT email account then you need to update the stored passwords in Thunderbird.

  • Settings > Privacy & Security
  • Scroll down to Passwords
  • click on 'Saved Passwords'
  • Click on 'Show Passwords'

There will be two lines for each account - one incoming either starting with 'mailbox:// or starting with 'imap://..' and one outgoing starting with smtp://. You need to do the following for both lines.

  • Right click on line and select 'Edit Password'
  • Completely clear all the contents
  • Carefully type the correct password
  • When all done, click on 'Close'

Exit Thunderbird and wait a few moments for background processes to complete. Start Thunderbird.
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
If the lockout is by windows file explorer, click to windows file explorer, click the view>show>hidden items. Normally, you can restore thunderbird with these steps:

  1. ensure thunderbird is installed and NOT running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should place you in appdata\roaming folder
  4. delete the thunderbird folder
  5. you should be in Appdata\roaming folder , copy&paste the Thunderbird folder from old PC.
  6. start thunderbird and all should be there

content:
Ak je QR kód vložený ako obrázok z internetu (nie priamo v prílohe), Thunderbird ho z bezpečnostných dôvodov blokuje.

1. Riešenie: Otvor e-mail Klikni hore na: „Zobraziť vzdialený obsah“

Alebo natrvalo: Nastavenia → Súkromie a bezpečnosť Povoliť „Načítať vzdialený obsah v správach“ = 2. Zobrazenie len ako „prostý text“: Ak máš zapnuté zobrazenie správ ako čistý text, obrázky (teda aj QR kód) sa nezobrazia. Skontroluj: Menu Zobraziť → Telo správy ako → Pôvodné HTML (alebo „Jednoduché HTML“)

content:
We would have no way of knowing any of your passwords. It would be a great security risk if we did. If you're looking for one of your email account passwords that is stored within Thunderbird, you can look in

<Tools | Settings | Privacy and Security | Passwords>

Otherwise, you may have to go to your email provider's web site and reset your password there, then update Thunderbird to reflect the new password.
